title: Git Bisect
description: Git bisect performs a binary search over commit history to locate the exact commit that introduced a bug. By marking commits as 'good' or 'bad', it halves the search space each step, finding the culprit in O(log n) steps even across thousands of commits.

title: Package Version Resolution
description: Package managers like npm and pip maintain sorted version lists for each package. When resolving version constraints (e.g., >=1.2.0 <2.0.0), they use binary search to efficiently find the highest compatible version from potentially hundreds of published releases.

title: Database B-Tree Lookup
description: Within each node of a B-tree index, databases perform binary search over the sorted keys to determine which child pointer to follow. This makes each level's lookup O(log k) where k is the branching factor, compounding efficiency across the tree's O(log n) height.
